entries.title: Nazareth peaking at the right time, advance to IHSA Class 4A state championship
entries.summary: <p>NORMAL, Ill. — Friday’s IHSA Class 4A semifinal game was the Stella Sakalas show.</p><p>The Nazareth senior forward was dominant from tip-off, scoring the first basket of the game and leaving her imprint on the game throughout in the Roadrunners’ 54-36 win over Belleville East to advance to the 4A state championship.</p><p>This will be Nazareth’s fourth straight appearance in the championship game. Coach Ed Stritzel said he had a discussion with Sakalas this morning about ending her Nazareth career at her best. Nazareth (34-3) exploited the Lancer’s lack of size by finding Sakalas in the post or running her off screens for a mid-range jumper. Sakalas scored 30 points on 13-for-17 shooting with five rebounds. </p><p>Whether it was her nifty drives to the basket, or knocking down jumpers from the elbow, Sakalas got whatever she wanted against the Lancers. At 6-0, Sakalas has the handle to blow past opposing forward and the height advantage to score over guards in the paint. </p><p>“I knew she'd step up and just have a monster game for us,” Stritzel said. “We wanted to get her some quicker shots.”</p><p>Nazareth wanted to score quickly to avoid having long, drawn-out possessions, which benefited the Lancers. That Sakalas was able to score so efficiently speaks to both her scoring prowess and her team's ability to find her in the right spots.</p><p>“I felt very comfortable out of the court, and even more comfortable with this set of girls,” Sakalas said. “So I think our ability to rebound and push the ball up the court really helped us today.”</p><p>Nazareth led by as many as 22 points and spent only 20 seconds without the lead. They scored 38 points in the paint. They held a 33-21 halftime lead but opened up the game in the third quarter, when the Roadrunners allowed just three points.</p><p>The Roadrunners’ zone defense is tough for opposing teams to score against. Nazareth is disciplined but opportunistic. The Roadrunners are so secure in the defensive scheme that they know the exact moment to pounce and jump the passing lanes. With their size and athleticism on the floor with Sakalas, junior forward Sam Austin and freshman forward Mia Gage, the Roadrunners can hound opposing teams. Nazareth turned 19 Lancer turnovers into 15 points.</p><p>“We knew which way they [liked to] pass,” said junior point guard Sophia Towne, who scored three points and dished out five assists. “So we [tried] to cut [off] those gaps.”</p><p>Gage scored 13 points and recorded three steals and two monster blocks. Austin scored five points, grabbed five rebounds and recorded four assists.</p><p>Friday’s win was a dominant wire-to-wire win by the Roadrunners. They played better as the game went on and once they seized control, they left little doubt that they were the best team on the floor on Friday.</p><p>“Once the game gets close, we just kind of lock into a ‘We're not losing mentality,’ and we just up the pace on defense because that's what sets up our offense,” Towne said.</p><p>With its appearance in the championship game clinched, Nazareth is playing its best basketball of the season. Sakalas, in particular, is finding her rhythm at the most pivotal time of the season.</p><p>“Our saying is, ‘Peak in March,’ and it's finally March,” Sakalas said. “We did all the work to get here and to be here, so I think just ending [the season] on a high note. We know what it's like to not end it on a high note [at CEFCU Arena].”<br></p>
entries.author: Kyle Williams

entries.title: Reality is immaterial in today's politics
entries.summary: <p>Unless you’ve been under a rock, you likely know that former President Bill Clinton seemed to say that he flew on Jeffrey Epstein’s private jet with JB Pritzker and his spouse — after saying that it was likely that Epstein as well as his notorious co-conspirator Ghislaine Maxwell were passengers on every flight he took on that plane.</p><p>Clinton made his remarks during a deposition taken by the U.S. House Oversight Committee last month. He was asked during the deposition about whether Epstein and Maxwell were on “every flight” he took on the jet. Clinton said "I think he was" about Epstein’s presence and "I think she was" about Maxwell’s.</p><p>The former Democratic president then went on to say, "I think it was on one of these trips, I think that I had my first trip for the man who’s now the governor of Illinois, JB Pritzker and his wife, they gave me, they helped me get started." The video snippet was released online by the "rapid response" unit of the Republican National Committee.</p><p>The story has no <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/illinois-governor/2026/03/03/jb-pritzker-bill-clinton-erroneous-testimony-jeffrey-epstein-aircraft" >factual support</a>.</p><div class="RichTextSidebarModule Enhancement" data-module data-align-center><a class="AnchorLink" id="module-040000" name="module-040000"></a> <div class="RichTextSidebarModule-title">Columnist </div> <div class="RichTextModule-items RichTextBody">Columnist</div> </div><p>Clinton and Epstein went separate ways in the early 2000s and the Clinton flights with the Pritzkers were in 2008 and 2013.</p><p>Pritzker has never shown up on flight logs for Epstein’s plane.</p><p>A top Clinton aide issued a correction to his boss’ comments that very night, and the Pritzker campaign issued a flat-out denial.</p><p>KSDK TV in St. Louis checked the flight logs for both the 2008 and 2013 Clinton/Pritzker trips and <a class="Link" href="https://www.ksdk.com/article/news/local/flight-records-court-records-contradict-claims-illinois-gov-pritzker-flew-on-jeffrey-epsteins-plane/63-15809848-1862-4bf4-8f47-695be87e36f0" target="_blank" >confirmed no Epstein connections</a> whatsoever.</p><p>But this is 2026. Reality matters less to more people than possibly ever before in politics. Both major parties have succumbed to fantasy worlds of their own making.</p><p>As just one example, a quick glance at Democratic primaries in this state at almost every level shows that some "true blue" Democrats are now regularly accused of being "MAGA supporters" on the flimsiest of pretenses.</p><p>Pritzker himself is funding a multi-million-dollar moral panic U.S. Senate campaign on behalf of his Lt. Gov. Juliana Stratton and against one of her rivals Democratic U.S. Rep. Raja Krishnamoorthi, which basically calls an immigrant a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement cheerleader who literally "sold us out."</p><p>State Sen. Willie Preston, D-Chicago, and his congressional campaign have been intensely pushing an insanely false story about how one of his congressional rivals sought out the support of the American Israel Public Affairs Committee. Sen. Robert Peters, D-Chicago, is Jewish, so maybe that’s why, but there is no way on this planet that the progressive senator wanted AIPAC’s support. It’s laughable on its face.</p><p>Thankfully, no mass media outlets have taken that seriously and neither should you. After we took a pass on the story, Preston’s spokesperson accused us of not treating his candidate fairly. In the real world, it ranked right up there as the dumbest story pitch of the campaign season.</p><p>Incumbent state Rep. Jaime Andrade, D-Chicago, is trying to fend off a progressive primary challenger who’s backed by the Chicago Teachers Union by attempting to paint his leftie Latino activist and community leader rival as a supporter of… wait for it… ICE. Yes, ICE. Miguel Alvelo-Rivera may be a lot of things, but an ICE supporter he most definitely ain’t.</p><p>And this is how Andrade justified the claims he’s made repeatedly in advertising to the Chicago Tribune: "Congresswoman [Delia] Ramirez supports Alvelo-Rivera; Ramirez has received campaign contributions from two California Democrats, U.S. Rep. Robert Garcia and House Democratic Chair Pete Aguilar; and those California lawmakers voted in 2024 for a resolution that included funding increases for parts of ICE."</p><p>Gimme a break.</p><p>This crud isn’t new, of course. We’ve had a problem in this country and in this state with hallucinatory campaigns that go beyond the normal campaign fibs for a very long time.</p><p>And connecting political rivals with pedophiles is also hardly new at the state level. Republican state legislative candidates who advocated for reducing state government mandates on school districts were regularly accused by Illinois House Democrats of opening the door for pedophiles and other dregs of society into children’s classrooms.</p><p>But a video of a former president saying he thinks he flew on Epstein’s jet with Pritzker and likely Epstein himself? Clinton did say what he said. The video is on the internet, and the internet is forever and quite strange. The fact that Pritzker’s wealthy cousin had close ties to Epstein will likely only fuel the fire.</p><p>It is undoubtedly false, but I doubt it’ll ever totally go away.</p><p><i>Rich Miller also publishes Capitol Fax, a daily political newsletter, and CapitolFax.com.</i></p><div class="RichTextSidebarModule Enhancement" data-module data-align-center><a class="AnchorLink" id="module-b50000" name="module-b50000"></a> <div class="RichTextModule-items RichTextBody">Send letters to <a class="Link" href="mailto:letters@suntimes.com" target="_blank" >letters@suntimes.com</a>. More about <a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/submitting-opinion-and-letters" >how to submit here</a>.</div> </div><p></p>
entries.author: Rich Miller

entries.title: Chicago police officer shot by Dexter Reed countersues estate
entries.summary: <p>The Chicago police officer shot in the wrist by Dexter Reed during a traffic stop in 2024, which prompted gunfire that killed Reed, is countersuing Reed’s estate.</p><p>Officer Gregory Saint Louis claims in the Thursday filing that he suffered physical and mental pain “that has greatly affected his career as a law enforcement officer and enjoyment of life.”</p><p>Reed, 26, was in his car March 21, 2024, in the 3800 block of West Ferdinand Street when five Harrison District tactical team officers stopped, approached his vehicle and ordered him to roll down his window. As officers continued to order Reed to roll down the window, Reed opened fire and struck Saint Louis, who was on the passenger side of the car, according to the city’s Civilian Office of Police Accountability.</p><p>The four other officers fired 96 shots in less than a minute, striking Reed 13 times. He was pronounced dead at Mount Sinai Hospital.</p><p>Reed’s family filed a federal civil rights lawsuit, claiming the officers unlawfully pulled over his SUV and were “outrageously” aggressive as they approached him. A City Council committee later rejected a $1.25 million settlement for the family.</p><p>In the counterclaim, lawyers for Saint Louis said the officer was taken to Stroger Hospital, where he underwent emergency surgery and further medical care for his gunshot wound.</p><p>Reed’s actions “were conducted willfully and maliciously with an utter disregard for the health, welfare, safety and life” of Saint Louis, his counterclaim said.</p><p>Saint Louis’s counterclaim alleges battery and intentional infliction of emotional distress against Reed. He is seeking compensatory damages for economic losses and any other relief for general damages.</p><p>Lawyers for Reed's family and Saint Louis could not be reached for comment.<br></p>
entries.author: Kade Heather

entries.title: Roseanne Tellez is latest casualty at Fox 32 Chicago after firing of Scott Schneider, executive producer
entries.summary: <p>Roseanne Tellez is the latest casualty to surface in the massive turnover at Fox 32. Her contract wasn’t renewed weeks ago, a Fox spokesperson confirmed Friday to the Sun-Times.</p><p>The news of her departure comes one day after the station fired anchor Scott Schneider and executive producer Marissa Rubino.</p><p>Tellez had been with Fox 32 since 2019, when she assumed morning anchor duties on "Good Day Chicago." She began in Chicago at WGN in 1990 during its superstation days, and she anchored from 1995 to 2004. She then moved to CBS 2, where she was a reporter and morning anchor for 15 years.</p><p>Schneider, who anchored the 5 and 9 p.m. newscasts, had been with the station for 10 years, and Rubino for almost seven. The Fox spokesperson confirmed their ousters, but sources were unaware of the reason for their dismissals. More departures aren’t expected.</p><p>“Marissa was an extremely hard-working and dedicated news producer who wore so many hats in the newsroom,” a source said. “It will be very hard to replace her.”</p><p>Fox 32 vice president of news content and programming Sean O’Heir has been working to remake the newsroom since taking the title in September. The station, which has struggled in the local ratings, hired four reporters and a producer in the last month.</p><p>Fox 32 is still without a lead sports anchor after Lou Canellis left for NBC 5 in late January. Cassie Carlson and Tina Nguyen appear to be sharing the role while the station seeks a potential hire. The job is not only in the nation's third-largest market, but it comes with a programming agreement with the Bears through 2027. </p><p>In January, O'Heir told the Sun-Times that he's planning for more local news and sports coverage on Fox Chicago+ (WPWR-Channel 50). </p><p>“More and more local programming, and I love all kinds of local sports,” O’Heir said then. “I’m going to put as much sports as I can on that channel, and in 2026, you’ll see more live local programming, sports and non-sports, on there.”</p><p>It aleady has been a volatile year for Chicago TV newscasts. NBC 5 fired sports anchor Mike Berman in January before hiring Canellis. Last month, WGN let go of nine on-air talents and several behind-the-scenes staffers as part of broadcaster Nexstar's nationwide cuts. Now Fox 32 is making changes.<br></p>
entries.author: Jeff Agrest

entries.title: Daylight saving time returns at 2 a.m. Sunday, remember to 'spring forward'
entries.summary: <p>Illinois and most of America “springs forward” at 2 a.m. Sunday for the start of daylight saving time. </p><p>Besides remembering to set any clocks an hour ahead that you have at home or in your car that still need to be changed manually, you might lose even more sleep if you think about how losing that hour of sleep can do more than leave you tired and cranky the next day. It also could harm your health.</p><p>Things to know about the time change:<br></p><ul class="rte2-style-ul" id="rte-4df8df52-19a5-11f1-8ab9-29ac55250791"><li>Darker mornings and more evening light knock your body clock out of whack — which can cause sleep trouble for weeks or even longer. Studies have even found an uptick in heart attacks and strokes right after the March time change.</li><li>There are ways to ease the adjustment, including getting more sunshine to help reset your circadian rhythm for healthful sleep.</li><li>Fatal car crashes temporarily jump the first few days after the spring time change, according to a study of U.S. traffic fatalities, especially in the morning, which researchers attributed to sleep deprivation.</li><li>Hawaii and most of Arizona don’t make the spring switch, sticking to standard time year-round — along with Puerto Rico, American Samoa, Guam and the U.S. Virgin Islands. Worldwide, dozens of countries also observe daylight saving time, starting and ending at different dates.</li><li>We'll return to Central Standard Time on Nov. 1, when clocks “fall back” as daylight saving time ends</li></ul>
entries.author: AP

entries.title: México sigue siendo un destino popular de ‘spring break’ a pesar de los confinamientos por la violencia del cártel
entries.summary: <p>El asesor de viajes Peter Carideo no se está alejando de recomendar México como destino para las vacaciones de primavera, incluso después de que la violencia relacionada con los cárteles provocó caos en una ciudad turística hace menos de dos semanas.</p><p>“Sin duda, [los clientes todavía] van a México”, dijo Carideo, un asesor de CRC Travel que trabaja principalmente con viajeros del Medio Oeste. “Inicialmente, hubo una avalancha de llamadas en un período de dos días para cancelar, cancelar, cancelar, pero eran para más adelante en ese mes”.</p><p>“Realmente no hemos visto la avalancha de cancelaciones que creo que estaban proyectando, así que, sí, la gente va a México. Tal vez no tanto a Puerto Vallarta, pero sí a Cabo, a Cancún”, afirmó Carideo.</p><div class="RichTextSidebarModule Enhancement" data-module data-align-center><a class="AnchorLink" id="module-410000" name="module-410000"></a> <div class="RichTextModule-items RichTextBody"><h3>La Voz Chicago WhatsApp</h3>Encuentra más noticias en nuestro canal de WhatsApp. <a class="Link" href="https://www.whatsapp.com/channel/0029Vb0XT1XCHDylm3rCs40P" target="_blank" ><b>Síguenos.</b></a></div> </div><p>Esta semana, la Embajada y los Consulados de Estados Unidos en México emitieron un <a class="Link" href="https://mx.usembassy.gov/message-to-u-s-citizens-spring-break-travel/" target="_blank" >aviso de viaje para las vacaciones de primavera</a>, advirtiendo a los viajeros que “los riesgos de crimen y secuestro permanecen” a pesar de que la violencia en el estado de Jalisco terminó hace más de una semana.</p><p>Los disturbios en Puerto Vallarta ocurrieron después de que el Ejército Mexicano matara el 22 de febrero al líder del Cártel de Jalisco Nueva Generación (CJNG), Nemesio Rubén Oseguera Cervantes, conocido como “El Mencho”. Estados Unidos emitió una orden de refugio en el lugar para los residentes allí, mientras que se ordenaron otros cierres durante al menos dos días.</p><p>El Departamento de Estado de Estados Unidos <a class="Link" href="https://travel.state.gov/en/international-travel/travel-advisories/mexico.html" target="_blank" >advierte</a> no viajar a seis estados mexicanos. Se insta a los viajeros a reconsiderar los viajes a siete estados más, mientras que la mayoría de los estados restantes están bajo un aviso de “ejercer una mayor precaución”. Los estados de Yucatán y Campeche figuran como “ejercer precauciones normales”.</p><p>Las aerolíneas reanudaron operaciones normales en los aeropuertos de Guadalajara y Puerto Vallarta dentro de un par de días después de la muerte de Oseguera Cervantes.</p><div class="Enhancement" data-align-center> <div class="Enhancement-item" data-crop=""> <figure class="Figure"><a class="AnchorLink" id="image-01bfc2" name="image-01bfc2"></a> <picture data-crop="medium"> <source type="image/webp" width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/cff8e91/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4032x2263+0+381/resize/490x275!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F94%2F37%2Fd7d007c943eb8595274ec8fcb696%2Fimg-2967.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/5470128/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4032x2263+0+381/resize/980x550!/format/webp/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F94%2F37%2Fd7d007c943eb8595274ec8fcb696%2Fimg-2967.jpg 2x" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <source width="490" height="275" data-srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/112a5f4/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4032x2263+0+381/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F94%2F37%2Fd7d007c943eb8595274ec8fcb696%2Fimg-2967.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" srcset="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" /> <img class="Image" alt="Un auto alquilado y un camión se incendian en una calle de Puerto Vallarta el 22 de febrero. El exdiputado estatal de Illinois, Jonathan “Yoni” Pizer, quien ahora trabaja para el congresista Mike Quigley, demócrata de Illinois, y sus pasajeros fueron sacados del vehículo a punta de pistola poco antes de que fuera incendiado." srcset="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/112a5f4/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4032x2263+0+381/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F94%2F37%2Fd7d007c943eb8595274ec8fcb696%2Fimg-2967.jpg 1x,https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/b8122b0/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4032x2263+0+381/resize/980x550!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F94%2F37%2Fd7d007c943eb8595274ec8fcb696%2Fimg-2967.jpg 2x" width="490" height="275" data-src="https://cst.brightspotcdn.com/dims4/default/112a5f4/2147483647/strip/true/crop/4032x2263+0+381/resize/490x275!/quality/90/?url=https%3A%2F%2Fchorus-production-cst-web.s3.us-east-1.amazonaws.com%2Fbrightspot%2F94%2F37%2Fd7d007c943eb8595274ec8fcb696%2Fimg-2967.jpg" data-lazy-load="true" src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3d3dy53My5vcmcvMjAwMC9zdmciIHZlcnNpb249IjEuMSIgaGVpZ2h0PSIyNzVweCIgd2lkdGg9IjQ5MHB4Ij48L3N2Zz4=" > </picture> <div class="Figure-content"><figcaption class="Figure-caption"><p>Un auto alquilado y un camión se incendian en una calle de Puerto Vallarta el 22 de febrero. El exdiputado estatal de Illinois, Jonathan “Yoni” Pizer, quien ahora trabaja para el congresista Mike Quigley, demócrata de Illinois, y sus pasajeros fueron sacados del vehículo a punta de pistola poco antes de que fuera incendiado.</p></figcaption><span class="line"></span><div class="Figure-credit"><p>Proporcionada</p></div></div> </figure> </div> </div><p>Michael Baney, gerente principal de inteligencia para Crisis24, agencia de gestión de riesgos de viaje, dijo que aunque es probable que las riñas entre cárteles continúen después de la muerte del líder del cártel, los turistas no son el objetivo de la violencia.</p><p>“Una nota importante es que, a lo largo de esta reciente violencia, no hemos visto a los combatientes de cárteles apuntar deliberadamente a extranjeros, y es poco probable que lo hagan en el futuro”, compartió Baney en un comunicado.</p><p>“Para los viajeros de las vacaciones de primavera, la conclusión clave es simple: la violencia de los cárteles es real, pero no está dirigida a los extranjeros. Al estar conscientes de su entorno y evitar los puntos críticos, puede reducir significativamente su riesgo”, continuó Baney.</p><p>Menos de una semana después de que Oseguera Cervantes fue asesinado, el ataque de Estados Unidos e Israel a Irán provocó ataques de represalia en todo el Medio Oriente, causando una gran incertidumbre en la región.</p><p>Stephen Scott, un asesor de viajes de Global Travel Collection, dijo que encontrar nuevos destinos para los clientes en las últimas dos semanas ha sido como jugar a un juego de “Whac-a-Mole”.</p><p>“Parece que cada semana tenemos algo nuevo que nos llega como asesores,” dijo Scott, quien está basado en Chicago y tiene aproximadamente la mitad de su clientela viviendo en el área de Chicago.</p><p>La violencia en Jalisco generó principalmente incertidumbre para sus clientes, quienes plantearon preguntas sobre el momento de sus viajes y los tipos de excursiones que planeaban. Comprender la geografía de México ha sido otra pregunta común de los viajeros, afirmó.</p><p>La mayoría de las personas que ya habían reservado viajes a México han ido, dijo Scott.</p><p>“La gente sigue viajando a México, continúan teniendo grandes vacaciones y las personas están allí ahora mismo pasándola increíble”, agregó Scott.</p><p>“Los huéspedes con los que hemos estado trabajando para otros viajes que no han reservado aún, han sido los que realmente han estado cambiando sus viajes, ya sea en su totalidad o ajustando el modo de viaje”, detalló Scott.</p><p>Aconsejó a los viajeros en áreas de México bajo ciertos avisos que planifiquen con anticipación. Scott recomienda reservar tours con empresas reputables que conozcan las áreas circundantes, en lugar de elegir un guía turístico a su llegada.</p><p><i>Traducido con una herramienta de inteligencia artificial (AI) y editado por </i><a class="Link" href="https://chicago.suntimes.com/la-voz" target="_blank" ><i>La Voz Chicago</i></a></p>
entries.author: Kade Heather
